[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getSemanticEvmVersion()] Can an unprivileged user submit a historical block query with a block number that maps to an older EVM version (e.g., block 44029065 on MAINNET mapping to EVM_VERSION_0_30 instead of EVM_VERSION_0_34), causing the EVM to execute with the wrong opcode set and produce incorrect execution records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getEvmVersionForBlock(long blockNumber)] Can an unprivileged user pass a negative block number to `getEvmVersionForBlock()` such that `getEvmVersions().floorEntry(blockNumber)` returns null and the fallback `EVM_VERSION` is used instead of the historically correct version, causing the mirror node to export records with incorrect EVM version metadata?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getEvmVersionForBlock(long blockNumber)] Can an unprivileged user query a historical block at index 0 on TESTNET (which has an empty `evmVersions` map in `HederaNetwork.TESTNET`), causing `getEvmVersions()` to fall back to `DEFAULT_EVM_VERSION_MAP` with only `(0L, EVM_VERSION)`, and thus always use the latest EVM version regardless of actual historical version, producing incorrect records?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getEvmVersions()] Can an unprivileged user on TESTNET, PREVIEWNET, or OTHER network trigger historical contract calls that always resolve to `DEFAULT_EVM_VERSION_MAP` (only entry `(0L, EVM_VERSION)`), causing all historical blocks on those networks to execute with the latest EVM version and export records with incorrect EVM version context to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` line in `buildTransactionProperties()` — where user-defined `properties` override all defaults — to inject a custom `contracts.sidecars` value that re-enables sidecar generation with unexpected types, causing incorrect or unexpected sidecar records to be exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override mechanism to inject a malicious `contracts.chainId` value, causing the EVM to execute transactions under a different chain ID and export records with an incorrect chain ID to the mirror node, breaking cross-chain integrity checks?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject a custom `ledger.id` value, causing the EVM to execute with a spoofed ledger identity and export records with an incorrect ledger ID to the mirror node, making those records appear to originate from a different network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject `contracts.evm.version` with a non-existent or malformed version string, causing the EVM executor to fail silently or use an unexpected version and export records with incorrect EVM version metadata to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to set `executor.disableThrottles` to `false`, re-enabling throttles and causing certain contract calls to be throttled/rejected, resulting in missing execution records exported to the mirror node for those calls?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to set `fees.simpleFeesEnabled` to `true`, causing fee calculations to change and export incorrect fee records to the mirror node that do not match the actual consensus node fee records?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to set `contracts.systemContract.scheduleService.scheduleCall.enabled` to `false`, causing schedule service contract calls to fail and export incorrect failure records to the mirror node instead of the expected success records?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject `nodes.nodeRewardsEnabled` as `false`, bypassing the deliberate reward-failure mechanism and causing node reward records to be incorrectly exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to set `nodes.minNodeRewardBalance` to a value other than `Long.MAX_VALUE`, causing the deliberate reward-failure condition to be bypassed and incorrect reward distribution records to be exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to set `tss.hintsEnabled` or `tss.historyEnabled` to `true`, causing TSS-related records to be generated and exported to the mirror node when they should not be, resulting in incorrect records?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject `jumboTransactions.allowedHederaFunctionalities` with additional transaction types (e.g., `CryptoTransfer`), causing those transaction types to be processed as jumbo transactions and export incorrect or unexpected records to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to set `contracts.throttle.throttleByGas` to `true`, causing gas-based throttling to activate and silently drop contract calls that would otherwise produce records, resulting in missing records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to set `contracts.throttle.throttleByOpsDuration` to `true`, causing operation-duration throttling to activate and cause certain contract calls to be rejected, resulting in missing records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject `hedera.realm` or `hedera.shard` with incorrect values, causing entity IDs in exported records to be computed with wrong realm/shard values, resulting in incorrect entity references in mirror node records?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject `contracts.maxRefundPercentOfGasLimit` with a value exceeding 100, bypassing the `@Max(100)` constraint that only applies to the `maxGasRefundPercentage` field, causing incorrect gas refund records to be exported to the mirror node?
